title: Lithocarpus eichleri
taxonomy: Lithocarpus eichleri is classified as a species within the genus Lithocarpus, which is a part of the beech family Fagaceae. The genus Lithocarpus is a group of evergreen trees native to Southeast Asia. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus eichleri shares characteristics with other beech family members, including oak and beech species. Within the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus eichleri is further classified as part of the subfamily Quercoideae. This classification is based on its botanical and morphological features.
uses: Lithocarpus eichleri has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its bark and leaves are believed to possess medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lithocarpus eichleri is sometimes used to treat fever and gastrointestinal issues. Its roots may also have antimicrobial properties, although these uses are not extensively documented. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the medicinal potential of Lithocarpus eichleri.

These compounds include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Lithocarpus eichleri, also known as the Eichler's Lithocarpus or Chinese Bur Oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential to aid in the management of various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus eichleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Lithocarpus eichleri is prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used in Chinese medicine to treat digestive issues and other health complaints.
